steps.title: Find the hypotenuse
steps.content:
The problem gives $AB=16$ and says $AC$ is $25$ greater than $AB$.
$$
AC=16+25=41
$$
Since angle $B$ is $90^\circ$, side $AC$ is the hypotenuse.

steps.title: Use equal area formulas
steps.content:
The area can be written using legs $AB$ and $BC$, or using hypotenuse $AC$ and altitude $BD$.
$$
\frac{1}{2}AB\cdot BC=\frac{1}{2}AC\cdot BD
$$
Cancel 
$$
\frac{1}{2}
$$ and divide by $BD\cdot AB$.
$$
\frac{BC}{BD}=\frac{AC}{AB}
$$
